task_completion.description: Navigate to or target the specified website for auditing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser, SEO crawler tool, or terminal script showing 'https://crawler-test.com/' as the active URL or crawl target.

task_completion.description: Crawl and identify image resources on the website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The auditing tool or script displaying a list of crawled resources that are images (e.g., filtered by extensions like .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .webp, .gif).

task_completion.description: Filter or identify images returning Client Error (4xx) status codes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface or script output showing HTTP status codes 404 or 403 specifically associated with the crawled image resources.

task_completion.description: Export the audit results as a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal command indicating the creation or saving of a file with a '.csv' extension.

task_completion.description: Save the CSV file to the correct directory with the required filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file manager, save dialog, or terminal showing the file saved in '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/' (or '~/Documents/SEO/exports/') and the filename containing the string 'broken_images'.

task_completion.description: Verify the contents of the exported CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opened CSV file (in a spreadsheet viewer, text editor, or terminal output) showing rows of image URLs from crawler-test.com alongside their 4xx status codes.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually audited the website.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an actual crawling process, network requests, or a script executing against crawler-test.com, rather than manually typing fake URLs into a file.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV file is a genuine result of the audit.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV file should appear to be a genuine export (e.g., contains proper headers, multiple rows of real crawled data) and not a manually constructed dummy file.

integrity.description: Verify that any script used does not hardcode the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: If a script was written to perform the task, its source code should show dynamic HTTP requests and parsing, not a hardcoded list of broken image URLs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://crawler-test.com/. Expected image extensions to look for: .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .gif, .webp. Expected client error status codes: 404, 403. Required export directory: /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports (or ~/Documents/SEO/exports). The exported filename must include 'broken_images' and be a CSV.
